content: During the preparation of NH3 in Haber's process, the promoter(s) used is/are -
explanation: During preparation of NH3 in Haber's process Mo as well mixture of Al2O3 and K2O are used as promoters.

content:
Extraction of a metal (M) from its sulphide ore (M2S) involves the following chemical reactions

$$2{M_2}S + 3{O_2}\buildrel {Heat} \over \longrightarrow 2{M_2}O + 2S{O_2} \uparrow $$

$${M_2}S + 2{M_2}O\buildrel {Heat} \over \longrightarrow 6M + S{O_2} \uparrow $$

The metal (M) may be
explanation:
The metal (M) is copper and the reactions are as follows:

$$2C{u_2}S + 3{O_2}\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow 2C{u_2}O + S{O_2} \uparrow $$

$$C{u_2}S + 2C{u_2}O\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow 6Cu + S{O_2} \uparrow $$

content: In the Bayer's process, the leaching of alumina is done by using
explanation:
In the Bayer's process, the leaching of alumina is done by using NaOH.

$$\mathop {A{l_2}{O_3}(s)}\limits_{Bauxite\,ore} + 2NaOH(aq)\buildrel {150^\circ C,\,35\,atm} \over \longrightarrow \mathop {2Na[Al{{(OH)}_4}]}\limits_{Sodium\,aluminate\,(so{\mathop{\rm lub}} le\,in\,water)} + 3{H_2}O$$

$$2Na[Al{(OH)_4}] + C{O_2} \to A{l_2}{O_3}.x{H_2}O + 2NaHC{O_3}$$

$$A{l_2}{O_3}.x{H_2}O(s)\buildrel {1470\,K} \over \longrightarrow \mathop {A{l_2}{O_3}(s)}\limits_{Pure\,alu\min a} + x{H_2}O(g)$$

So, the option (b) is correct answer.

content: A copper coin was electroplated with Zn and then heated at high temperature until there is a change in colour. What will be the resulting colour?
explanation:
A copper coin was electroplated with zinc (Zn) and then heated at high temperature until there is a change in colour. The resulting colour will be golden. The golden colour is due to the zinc migrating through the copper to form the alpha-form of brass alloy (percentage of Cu > 65% and that of Zn > 35%).

$$Zn + Cu\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow Brass$$

Thus, the option (d) is correct answer.

content: The metal which can be used to obtain metallic Cu from aqueous CuSO<sub>4</sub> solution is
explanation:
Iron (Fe) is used to obtain Cu-metal from CuSO4 (aq) because Fe is more reactive than Cu (and also easily available and is cheaper) thus, replace the copper from CuSO4 to give copper (Cu).

CuSO4(aq) + Fe(s) $$\to$$ FeSO4(aq) + Cu(s)
